title: What if the First Impact had been the other way around
display_title: What if the First Impact had been the other way around
wikitext:
[]: What if the First Impact had been the other way around?             Quote Post by Replicator » Wed Oct 17, 2012 9:21 am This question kind of borders on fan-fiction, but I've been thinking about it for a while. If the First Impact, where Lilith's Black Moon collided with the Earth, resulting in the loss of its Spear of Longinus, and incapacitating Adam, who presumbaly arrived earlier, giving way to the spread of Lilith-based life, rather the Angels... What if it were the other way around? Let's say Lilith reached the Earth first, and then Adam crashed, and lost its Spear of Longinus, and the Angels populated the Earth instead of Humans/Lilin? Then, (probably in an alternate Dead Sea Scrolls, Lilith was stated to awaken at some indeterminate time, like Adam was supposed to in NGE, or unforseen events occur) Lilith is activated, and spreads LCL across the Earth. After billions of years, the Lilin arise. Would Lilith-based life stand any chance against an Angel-dominant ecosystem? Or maybe it probably wouldn't even got to the stage where Humans come around? Discuss...

[]:             Quote Post by Quadhonk » Wed Oct 17, 2012 9:24 am Since the Adam based life forms lacked the fruit of knowledge I doubt they would be motivated to pull the lance from Lillith. Since the lance would have impaled her once Adam crashed onto Earth. This would simply result in a planet dominated by Adam. Even in the case that Lillith based life emerges I don't think it would have much of a chance. Humanity could barely handle the Angels that we had in NGE, much less a whole planet of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Wed Oct 17, 2012 10:35 am If it had been the other way around, I think once Lilith awoke, her spear would have activated and corrected the mistake. (Since the angels would have lacked the knowledge to take precautions against this as said in the above post)

[]:             Quote Post by Gtamor » Wed Oct 17, 2012 11:00 am Angels were able to fight against lilin after millions of years of evolution. I doubt that lilin could ever reach a point where it could pose any serious thread against angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 17, 2012 11:41 am I think it's unlikely the Angels would revive Lilith, but if they did she'd tang their asses and do her own thing. How would they stop her?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Oct 17, 2012 4:01 pm Bagheera wrote: I think it's unlikely the Angels would revive Lilith, but if they did she'd tang their asses and do her own thing. How would they stop her?
